title: Python for AI
description: Learn the Python basics you need for AI tooling, data handling, and small production APIs.
outcomes: Set up Python environments and run scripts in a repeatable way.
Use Python primitives to model and manipulate practical data.
Write clean functions and classes for AI-facing helpers.
Read and write files safely for simple data pipelines.
Use APIs and simple web frameworks to build AI-ready backends.
Run code in notebooks and structure a small app prototype with FastAPI.

lessons.title: Python Setup
lessons.description: Install Python and understand how to run a script safely.
checklist: I can run a hello-world script.
I can check python and pip versions.
I can create a clean project directory.
questions: What problem does a Python virtual environment solve?
How do you verify you are running the expected Python version?

lessons.title: Variables and Data Types
lessons.description: Store and convert values using Python's core types.
checklist: I can define and update variables.
I can choose the correct type for simple cases.
I can describe immutable vs mutable behavior at a high level.
questions: Why should you keep data shapes consistent in AI code?
Where does Python's type behavior surprise teams during prototyping?

lessons.title: Lists and Dictionaries
lessons.description: Store collections and map keys to values for AI workflows.
checklist: I can filter and loop over a list.
I can represent structured data in dictionaries.
I can avoid common key/index mistakes.
questions: When does a dictionary outperform a list?
Which container is easier for one-to-one mappings?

lessons.title: Functions
lessons.description: Build reusable logic with parameters, return values, and docstrings.
checklist: I can define a function with input and output.
I can call helper functions from another function.
I can test a function using simple sample inputs.
questions: What is the difference between a function and a class method?
How do functions help with AI prompt and API consistency?

lessons.title: Classes
lessons.description: Represent domain objects with simple class structures.
checklist: I can define a class with attributes and methods.
I can instantiate objects for one use case.
I can explain when a class is overkill.
questions: When does a class make code easier to reason about?
Why should AI-facing models stay focused on one responsibility?

lessons.title: Files
lessons.description: Load and write text-based data safely with Python file APIs.
checklist: I can read and write files with context managers.
I can handle common text parsing issues.
I can validate input before writing output.
questions: Why is context manager syntax important for file handling?
What do you do when input format changes unexpectedly?

lessons.title: APIs
lessons.description: Call and consume external APIs safely and predictably.
checklist: I can send a basic request and inspect the response.
I can parse JSON into Python objects.
I can add error handling for failed requests.
questions: How do status codes change how you proceed?
How does timeout handling affect production reliability?

lessons.title: Virtual Environments
lessons.description: Use isolated environments so projects stay reproducible.
checklist: I can create and activate a venv.
I can pin dependencies in requirements.
I can explain why global installs are risky.
questions: Why do reproducible environments matter for team work?
What breaks when two apps depend on different package versions?

lessons.title: Jupyter Notebooks
lessons.description: Explore interactive notebooks for experiments and AI exploration.
checklist: I can run cells in sequence and inspect outputs.
I can keep imports, data prep, and results separate.
I can export or summarize a reproducible notebook.
questions: When is a notebook good for exploration but not production?
How do you keep notebooks maintainable for others?

lessons.title: FastAPI Introduction
lessons.description: Build a simple API route to serve AI-ready outputs.
checklist: I can run a tiny FastAPI app with one endpoint.
I can test requests and read JSON responses.
I can describe request/response boundaries.
questions: How would you expose a model helper through API endpoints?
What validation is important before returning AI output?

lessons.title: Mini Project: Prompt Utility Toolkit
lessons.description: Create a tiny CLI utility for reusable text-clean and request helper functions.
checklist: I can define a small input pipeline.
I can handle missing file paths and bad input.
I can explain how this utility helps an AI feature.
questions: What inputs does your utility accept?
How do you avoid silent failure in automation scripts?

lessons.title: Python for AI Starter API
lessons.description: Design a minimal FastAPI service contract for an AI workflow.
checklist: I can sketch endpoint contracts before coding.
I can define safe error messages and response shape.
I can list one security and one scalability concern.
questions: Which endpoint should start your API and why?
What one guardrail prevents unsafe input from reaching your model helper?

lessons.title: Python Foundations Quiz
lessons.description: Check readiness for moving into data-focused AI modules.
checklist: I can explain Python data organization.
I can read and describe API request/response behavior.
I can define a small reproducible setup.
questions: What is the difference between script style code and reusable function design?
Why are environments essential in team AI work?

quizQuestions.prompt: What is your best reason for using a virtual environment for an AI project?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: A virtual environment keeps dependencies isolated so each project can use exact versions without conflicts, improving reproducibility and reliability in teams.

options.label: Isolated project dependencies
options.explanation: A virtual environment keeps dependencies isolated so each project can use exact versions without conflicts, improving reproducibility and reliability in teams.

options.label: Global package installs
options.explanation: Global installs make projects harder to reproduce and can create dependency conflicts across teams.

options.label: One-cell prototypes
options.explanation: Notebooks are useful for exploration, but reliable AI workflows need reusable, testable code.

options.label: Trust successful responses
options.explanation: A successful request can still return malformed, incomplete, stale, or unsafe data.

quizQuestions.prompt: How do Python types help reduce mistakes when preparing AI inputs?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Clear types help you validate assumptions about input shape and format, catching inconsistencies before they reach model calls or API boundaries.

options.label: One-cell prototypes
options.explanation: Notebooks are useful for exploration, but reliable AI workflows need reusable, testable code.

options.label: Input shape and format validation
options.explanation: Clear types help you validate assumptions about input shape and format, catching inconsistencies before they reach model calls or API boundaries.

options.label: Trust successful responses
options.explanation: A successful request can still return malformed, incomplete, stale, or unsafe data.

options.label: Everything as strings
options.explanation: String-only data hides type mistakes and makes validation harder before AI inputs are sent downstream.

quizQuestions.prompt: When would you use a dictionary over a list in prompt/metadata workflows?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Use a dictionary when you need key-based access to named fields like user_id, text, timestamps, or route metadata, where order is less important than lookup and clarity.

options.label: Trust successful responses
options.explanation: A successful request can still return malformed, incomplete, stale, or unsafe data.

options.label: Everything as strings
options.explanation: String-only data hides type mistakes and makes validation harder before AI inputs are sent downstream.

options.label: Keyed metadata access
options.explanation: Use a dictionary when you need key-based access to named fields like user_id, text, timestamps, or route metadata, where order is less important than lookup and clarity.

options.label: Write before validation
options.explanation: File output from user data needs validation to avoid overwrites, leaks, or unsafe paths.

quizQuestions.prompt: Why does one small function matter before building AI features?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Small focused functions separate concerns, make behavior testable, and keep prompt preparation, request handling, and response parsing easier to maintain.

options.label: Everything as strings
options.explanation: String-only data hides type mistakes and makes validation harder before AI inputs are sent downstream.

options.label: Small reusable behavior units
options.explanation: Small focused functions separate concerns, make behavior testable, and keep prompt preparation, request handling, and response parsing easier to maintain.

options.label: Write before validation
options.explanation: File output from user data needs validation to avoid overwrites, leaks, or unsafe paths.

options.label: Model call first
options.explanation: The API shape should define validation, errors, observability, and reliability boundaries.

quizQuestions.prompt: When is a class a good fit in AI application code?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: A class helps when multiple fields and behaviors belong together, such as request context and helper methods, and you need reusable structured handling.

options.label: Write before validation
options.explanation: File output from user data needs validation to avoid overwrites, leaks, or unsafe paths.

options.label: Model call first
options.explanation: The API shape should define validation, errors, observability, and reliability boundaries.

options.label: Grouped state and behavior
options.explanation: A class helps when multiple fields and behaviors belong together, such as request context and helper methods, and you need reusable structured handling.

options.label: Global package installs
options.explanation: Global installs make projects harder to reproduce and can create dependency conflicts across teams.

quizQuestions.prompt: What should you do before writing file output from user-generated data?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Validate and normalize data first, handle encoding and missing fields, then write atomically or with explicit paths to avoid corrupting prior output.

options.label: Model call first
options.explanation: The API shape should define validation, errors, observability, and reliability boundaries.

options.label: Global package installs
options.explanation: Global installs make projects harder to reproduce and can create dependency conflicts across teams.

options.label: Validate before writing files
options.explanation: Validate and normalize data first, handle encoding and missing fields, then write atomically or with explicit paths to avoid corrupting prior output.

options.label: One-cell prototypes
options.explanation: Notebooks are useful for exploration, but reliable AI workflows need reusable, testable code.

quizQuestions.prompt: What are two checks you do before trusting a third-party API response?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Verify status codes and parse/validate response schema before using the payload, and add retry or fail-safe logic for temporary outages.

options.label: Global package installs
options.explanation: Global installs make projects harder to reproduce and can create dependency conflicts across teams.

options.label: One-cell prototypes
options.explanation: Notebooks are useful for exploration, but reliable AI workflows need reusable, testable code.

options.label: Status and schema validation
options.explanation: Verify status codes and parse/validate response schema before using the payload, and add retry or fail-safe logic for temporary outages.

options.label: Trust successful responses
options.explanation: A successful request can still return malformed, incomplete, stale, or unsafe data.

quizQuestions.prompt: Why can global package installs hurt AI teams?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Global installs mix libraries across projects, causing version conflicts and non-reproducible behavior between dev machines and deployments.

options.label: One-cell prototypes
options.explanation: Notebooks are useful for exploration, but reliable AI workflows need reusable, testable code.

options.label: Trust successful responses
options.explanation: A successful request can still return malformed, incomplete, stale, or unsafe data.

options.label: Everything as strings
options.explanation: String-only data hides type mistakes and makes validation harder before AI inputs are sent downstream.

options.label: Reproducible dependency isolation
options.explanation: Global installs mix libraries across projects, causing version conflicts and non-reproducible behavior between dev machines and deployments.

quizQuestions.prompt: What is a practical reason to move from notebook to scripts after prototyping?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Scripts and services are easier to version, test, and deploy reliably once logic is stable, while notebooks are great for exploration and iteration.

options.label: Move stable logic to scripts
options.explanation: Scripts and services are easier to version, test, and deploy reliably once logic is stable, while notebooks are great for exploration and iteration.

options.label: Trust successful responses
options.explanation: A successful request can still return malformed, incomplete, stale, or unsafe data.

options.label: Everything as strings
options.explanation: String-only data hides type mistakes and makes validation harder before AI inputs are sent downstream.

options.label: Write before validation
options.explanation: File output from user data needs validation to avoid overwrites, leaks, or unsafe paths.

quizQuestions.prompt: How does API shape support AI reliability?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: A clear request/response contract lets clients know what to send and what to expect, making AI helper services easier to debug, monitor, and evolve safely.

options.label: Clear request-response contracts
